record.field: Search records in an Odoo model using domain filters. Returns matching records with specified fields. Use this to find records that match certain criteria.
Read specific records by their IDs with selected fields. Use when you already know exact record IDs from a previous search or reference.
Create one or more records in an Odoo model. Use 'values' for a single record or 'values_list' for batch creation. Returns created record IDs.
Update existing records by IDs with new field values. All specified records receive the same values.
Delete records by IDs. This is a destructive operation — use with caution. Consider archiving (write active=false) instead of deleting when possible.
Count records matching a domain filter without fetching data. Efficient for checking how many records match criteria.

Group records by fields and compute aggregates (sum, count, avg, min, max). Use for analytics: totals by partner, counts by status, revenue by month. Specify groupby fields and optional aggregate expressions in fields (e.g. 'amount_total:sum', 'id:count_distinct'). Date/datetime fields support granularity: 'date:month', 'date:year', 'date:week'.

Get default field values for a model before creating a record. Helps understand required fields, auto-filled values, and proper field formats. Always call this before creating a record in an unfamiliar model.
List Odoo modules filtered by keyword and state. Use to discover available functionality and installed apps.

Call a public method on model records. Only public methods (not starting with _) are allowed. Use method='get_methods' with no ids to discover available methods on any model. Common patterns: action_confirm, action_done, button_validate, action_cancel, action_draft.

List companies accessible to the current user. Returns the current active company and all accessible companies. Use company IDs with context={'allowed_company_ids': [id]} on create/write/method_call tools to operate in a specific company.
